title: Add bounded in-memory session retention controls
description:
Add opt-in bounded in-memory session retention controls for _SESSIONS.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Retention is disabled by default and existing TPS/session behavior remains compatible.
- HERMES_TPS_MAX_SESSIONS prunes the in-memory session dictionary to the configured bound.
- HERMES_TPS_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS prunes stale sessions without real sleeps.
- Pruned sessions return the existing zero-value stats shape and are not recreated by reads.
- Retention policy is documented in observability contract diagnostics without sensitive identifiers.
- No external dependencies, background threads, package manager commands, or Hermes core changes are required.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Retention is disabled by default and existing TPS/session behavior remains compatible.
- [x] HERMES_TPS_MAX_SESSIONS prunes the in-memory session dictionary to the configured bound.
- [x] HERMES_TPS_SESSION_TTL_SECONDS prunes stale sessions without real sleeps.
- [x] Pruned sessions return the existing zero-value stats shape and are not recreated by reads.
- [x] Retention policy is documented in observability contract diagnostics without sensitive identifiers.
- [x] No external dependencies, background threads, package manager commands, or Hermes core changes are required.
notes: COMPLETED: implemented bounded in-memory session retention controls; verified tests and lint

title: Add per-IP rate limiting middleware to REST API endpoints to protect SQLite backend from burst requests
description:
Add per-IP rate limiting middleware to REST API endpoints to protect the SQLite backend from burst requests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Burst requests from one IP to protected REST endpoints receive HTTP 429 with Retry-After before handler/store work.
- Under-limit requests preserve existing REST response contracts.
- TPSConfig supports requests_per_minute and burst_size via defaults, TOML, env, and context overrides with validation.
- /api/v1/health remains exempt from throttling.
- Prometheus exposes tps_api_rate_limited_total after throttled requests when prometheus_client is available.
- Affected API/config/rate-limiting/Prometheus tests pass without new dependencies.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Burst requests from one IP to protected REST endpoints receive HTTP 429 with Retry-After before handler/store work.
- Under-limit requests preserve existing REST response contracts.
- TPSConfig supports requests_per_minute and burst_size via defaults, TOML, env, and context overrides with validation.
- /api/v1/health remains exempt from throttling.
- Prometheus exposes tps_api_rate_limited_total after throttled requests when prometheus_client is available.
- Affected API/config/rate-limiting/Prometheus tests pass without new dependencies.
notes: COMPLETED: Added per-IP rate limiting middleware, TPSConfig rate limit settings, Prometheus throttling counter, and focused tests. Verified with affected pytest suites.
close_reason: implemented, verified
agent_context: {"objective":"Add per-IP rate limiting middleware to all REST API endpoints so burst requests cannot overwhelm the SQLite backend or degrade monitoring reliability.","constraints":{"MUST":["Do not change existing endpoint response contracts for non-rate-limited requests","Return proper HTTP 429 responses with Retry-After header when limits exceeded","Make rate limits configurable via TPSConfig (requests_per_minute, burst_size)","Expose a Prometheus counter for rate-limited requests (tps_api_rate_limited_total)","Do not add external dependencies (no Redis, no slowapi — use stdlib/collections-based sliding window"],"SHOULD":["Use a simple sliding window counter per IP address","Clean up stale IP entries to prevent memory leaks","Log rate limit events at DEBUG level","Support exempting health endpoint from rate limiting"]},"files":{"allowed":["api.py","config.py","prometheus_metrics.py","tests/test_api.py","tests/test_config.py","tests/test_rate_limiting.py"],"forbidden":[".beads/beads.db",".env.local","credentials","plan.md","tasks.md","context-capsule.md"]}}

title: Add machine-readable observability contract endpoint for TPS snapshots, API responses, and Prometheus metrics metadata
description:
Add a machine-readable observability contract endpoint/helper that describes TPS status snapshots, API helper payloads, and optional REST/WebSocket/Prometheus metadata for consumers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Machine-readable observability contract helper returns contract version, plugin metadata, compatibility guidance, status snapshot schema, API/helper schema, WebSocket metadata, and Prometheus metadata.
- Status snapshot metadata documents last_tps, avg_tps, peak_tps, output_tokens, updated_at, updated_monotonic, and session_id semantics.
- Optional REST/WebSocket/Prometheus surfaces are represented explicitly as unavailable when their modules are absent on this branch.
- Existing register(), _on_post_api_request(), and get_tps_stats() behavior remains backward compatible.
- Focused tests and smoke check verify contract shape, JSON serializability, representative fields, optional-surface availability metadata, and no session-state mutation.
- README documents helper usage, versioning, unknown-field tolerance, freshness/session mismatch guidance, and Prometheus cardinality guidance.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria

- Machine-readable observability contract helper returns contract version, plugin metadata, compatibility guidance, status snapshot schema, API/helper schema, WebSocket metadata, and Prometheus metadata.
- Status snapshot metadata documents last_tps, avg_tps, peak_tps, output_tokens, updated_at, updated_monotonic, and session_id semantics.
- Optional REST/WebSocket/Prometheus surfaces are represented explicitly as unavailable when their modules are absent on this branch.
- Existing register(), _on_post_api_request(), and get_tps_stats() behavior remains backward compatible.
- Focused tests and smoke check verify contract shape, JSON serializability, representative fields, optional-surface availability metadata, and no session-state mutation.
- README documents helper usage, versioning, unknown-field tolerance, freshness/session mismatch guidance, and Prometheus cardinality guidance.
notes: COMPLETED: Added static get_observability_contract helper, tests, README docs, completion evidence, and independent approval review.

title: Add configurable privacy redaction for TPS identifiers across observability outputs
description:
Add configurable privacy redaction for TPS observability identifiers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared redaction policy governs session_id, model, provider, and configured identifier-like fields.
- Disabled mode preserves existing snapshot, helper, registration, and contract behavior.
- Enabled mode prevents raw covered identifiers from leaving available outbound surfaces.
- Deterministic pseudonyms are stable and non-raw.
- Salt/secret material is never emitted.
- Documentation and tests cover privacy behavior.
notes: COMPLETED: implemented shared configurable privacy redaction for TPS identifiers, wired current outbound surfaces, documented behavior, and verified with tests/smoke/bead hygiene
close_reason: implemented privacy redaction; pytest tests/test_privacy.py tests/test_api.py tests/test_hook.py passed (32 passed); smoke and br lint passed
agent_context: {"objective":"Add a configurable privacy redaction layer for TPS identifiers so session_id, model, provider, and exported metadata can be safely shared through status snapshots, REST/WebSocket responses, Prometheus labels, dashboard displays, logs, and historical exports without leaking raw identifiers by default when privacy mode is enabled.","constraints":{"MUST":["Provide a single configurable redaction policy covering session_id, model, provider, and any future identifier-like fields","Support deterministic pseudonymous values for grouping without exposing raw identifiers","Preserve existing defaults and public response contracts when privacy mode is disabled","Apply redaction consistently before data leaves trusted in-process state through API responses, WebSocket broadcasts, Prometheus labels, dashboard JSON, exports, and logs","Avoid external dependencies and keep hook-path overhead low","Document which fields are raw, redacted, hashed, or omitted under each mode"],"SHOULD":["Use a small helper module/function so all surfaces share one implementation","Support allowlist/denylist semantics or per-field mode overrides","Include tests for deterministic pseudonyms, disabled-mode backward compatibility, and coverage of every outbound surface","Expose redaction mode in diagnostics or observability contract metadata without revealing secrets"]},"files":{"allowed":["__init__.py","config.py","api.py","dashboard.py","prometheus_metrics.py","store.py","README.md","tests/"],"forbidden":[".beads/beads.db",".env.local","credentials","secrets","plan.md","tasks.md","context-capsule.md"]}}
